top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 106, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GranFondo San Sebastián's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
93ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
9,843ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GranFondo San Sebastián. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 9,843ft of climbing on basque Country coastal and inland climbing does to your power file on race day.
GranFondo San Sebastián's basque Country coastal and inland climbing forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 106 miles.
At GranFondo San Sebastián, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
106 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 106 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 106 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is GranFondo San Sebastián's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 106 miles on basque Country coastal and inland climbing. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GranFondo San Sebastián’s 106 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 106 miles of basque country coastal and inland climbing in San Sebastián (Donostia), Basque Country, Spain.
